```python
labelmapVolumeNode = getNode("label")
seg = slicer.mrmlScene.AddNewNodeByClass("vtkMRMLSegmentationNode")
slicer.modules.segmentations.logic().ImportLabelmapToSegmentationNode(labelmapVolumeNode, seg)
seg.CreateClosedSurfaceRepresentation()
slicer.mrmlScene.RemoveNode(labelmapVolumeNode)
```

The last line is optional. It removes the original labelmap volume so that the same information is not shown twice.

def applyThreshold(
    thresholdMin,
    thresholdMax,
    segmentationNode=None,
    segmentId=None,
    sourceVolumeNode=None,
):
    """
    Apply the Threshold effect to a segment.

    Parameters
    ----------
    thresholdMin : float
        Lower bound of the intensity range.
    thresholdMax : float
        Upper bound of the intensity range.
    segmentationNode : vtkMRMLSegmentationNode, optional
        Target segmentation node. Resolved by name if None.
    segmentId : str, optional
        ID of the segment to fill. Use the currently selected segment if None.
    sourceVolumeNode : vtkMRMLScalarVolumeNode, optional
        Source volume whose intensities drive the threshold.
        Resolved by name if None.
    """
    # --- Resolve nodes by name if not provided ---
    if segmentationNode is None:
        segmentationNode = slicer.mrmlScene.GetFirstNodeByClass("vtkMRMLSegmentationNode")
        if segmentationNode is None:
            raise RuntimeError("No vtkMRMLSegmentationNode found in the scene.")
    if sourceVolumeNode is None:
        sourceVolumeNode = slicer.mrmlScene.GetFirstNodeByClass("vtkMRMLScalarVolumeNode")
        if sourceVolumeNode is None:
            raise RuntimeError("No vtkMRMLScalarVolumeNode found in the scene.")

    # --- Set up the segment editor parameter set node ---
    segmentEditorSingletonTag = "SegmentEditor"
    parameterSetNode = slicer.mrmlScene.GetSingletonNode(
        segmentEditorSingletonTag, "vtkMRMLSegmentEditorNode"
    )
    if parameterSetNode is None:
        parameterSetNode = slicer.mrmlScene.CreateNodeByClass("vtkMRMLSegmentEditorNode")
        parameterSetNode.UnRegister(None)
        parameterSetNode.SetSingletonTag(segmentEditorSingletonTag)
        slicer.mrmlScene.AddNode(parameterSetNode)

def activateThresholdEffectForMovingSegmentation():
    # Find the moving segmentation node by fuzzy name match
    segmentationNode = None
    for node in slicer.mrmlScene.GetNodesByClass("vtkMRMLSegmentationNode"):
        if "moving" in node.GetName().lower():
            segmentationNode = node
            break
    if segmentationNode is None:
        raise RuntimeError("STATE_NOT_APPLIED: Moving segmentation node not found in scene")

    # Get the segment editor widget
    segmentEditorWidget = slicer.modules.segmenteditor.widgetRepresentation().self().editor

    # Set the segmentation node in the editor
    segmentEditorWidget.setSegmentationNode(segmentationNode)

    # Verify the segmentation node was set
    currentSegNode = segmentEditorWidget.segmentationNode()
    if currentSegNode != segmentationNode:
        raise RuntimeError("STATE_NOT_APPLIED: segmentation node not set in editor")

    # Find the Threshold effect by name
    thresholdEffect = segmentEditorWidget.effectByName("Threshold")
    if thresholdEffect is None:
        raise RuntimeError("STATE_NOT_APPLIED: Threshold effect not found - is the Segment Editor module loaded?")

    # Activate the Threshold effect
    segmentEditorWidget.setActiveEffect(thresholdEffect)

    # Verify the effect was activated
    activeEffect = segmentEditorWidget.activeEffect()
    if activeEffect is None or activeEffect.name() != "Threshold":
        raise RuntimeError("STATE_NOT_APPLIED: Threshold effect not activated")
